maxContractTerm.value: 4 years initial term, with discretionary extension of up to 4 additional years at term-end without rebid (effective ceiling 8 years through a 4 + 4 structure). Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31(3) states (verbatim, confirmed against the Mississippi Department of Education compilation): "Contracts shall be made for four (4) years, at the discretion of the local school board and shall include the stipulation for the placement of district-identifying signage on all buses under contract, as required under Section 37-41-43. Any and all bids may be rejected. At the expiration of any transportation contract, if the school board believes a route should remain substantially as established and finds that the carrier thereon has rendered efficient and satisfactory services it may extend the contract for not more than four (4) years, subject, however, to the provisions of Section 37-41-29." The four-year extension is a one-time, board-discretionary option, not an automatic or evergreen renewal. No longer-term authority was located in the education code, in Title 31 Chapter 7 (Public Purchases), or in administrative regulations. The Mississippi multi-year-contract authority for school personnel (Miss. Code Ann. § 37-9-25) governs superintendents/principals/licensed employees and does not extend to transportation contracts.

rebidRequired.value: Conditional. The default rule under § 37-41-31(1) requires that initial private pupil-transportation contracts be let to the lowest responsible bidder upon receipt of sealed bids or proposals, after the time, place, and manner of letting have been duly advertised in a newspaper in the county in accordance with the procedures in § 31-7-13(c). At the expiration of a contract, § 37-41-31(3) expressly authorizes the school board to extend the same contract for not more than four (4) additional years without a new bid IF (a) the board believes the route should remain substantially as established, and (b) finds the carrier has rendered efficient and satisfactory services. After any such extension expires (or if the board does not extend), a new competitive sealed-bid process is required to award a new contract. Exception under § 37-41-31(2): private contracts for the transportation of exceptional children (as defined in § 37-23-3) may be negotiated by the local school board without advertising or taking of bids, and the same exception applies under extraordinary circumstances where regular transportation is impractical, subject to compliance with State Board of Education transportation regulations. The Title 31, Chapter 7 general-procurement statute (§ 31-7-13) is cross-referenced only for the advertising procedure and does not displace the school-specific bid/extension rule.
rebidRequired.citation: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31(1)-(3) (2024); cross-reference to Miss. Code Ann. § 31-7-13(c) for advertising procedure; Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-29 governs contract content and per-pupil funding cap

renewalMechanics.value: Statutory discretionary extension only. Under § 37-41-31(3), at expiration of a transportation contract the local school board may extend the same contract "for not more than four (4) years" without rebidding, provided the board (i) believes the route should remain substantially as established, and (ii) finds the existing carrier has rendered efficient and satisfactory services. Any such extension remains subject to the provisions of § 37-41-29, which requires (a) a written contract filed with the superintendent of schools, (b) specified minimum contract contents — cost of services (including bus routes and field-trip costs), length of contract, terms for utilization of existing pupil-transportation facilities and existing fleet of buses (if applicable), required types and amounts of insurance limits (including Workers' Compensation), 30-day prior notice of cancellation on certificates of insurance, district-identifying signage as required under § 37-41-43, compliance with Mississippi Minimum Standards for School Buses and driver certification by the State Department of Education, and (c) caps per-pupil state funding for contract transportation at the amount allowed per pupil for public transportation in the same county and district. The statute does NOT provide for automatic/evergreen renewal, CPI/index adjustments, fuel escalators, or state-agency approval of renewals; the extension decision is committed to the local school board's discretion. For exceptional-children contracts under § 37-41-31(2), the local school board may negotiate (and thus may also renegotiate) without bidding, subject to compliance with State Board of Education transportation regulations; § 37-41-29(4) additionally exempts those contracts from filing with the State Board of Education.
renewalMechanics.citation: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31(3) (2024) (extension mechanism); Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-29 (2024) (contract content and per-pupil funding cap); Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31(2) (2024) (negotiated procurement exception for exceptional children)

statutoryCitation.value: Primary: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31 (2024) — Awarding of private transportation contracts (term, extension, and competitive-bid rule). Companion: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-29 (2024) — Private transportation of pupils; contents of contract; per-pupil funding cap; § 37-41-29(4) (special-education filing exemption). Cross-reference for advertising procedure: Miss. Code Ann. § 31-7-13(c) (2024). Definitional cross-reference: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-23-3 ("exceptional child" defined). Section history through Laws, 2014, ch. 486, HB 72, § 3 (eff. 7/1/2014). The Mississippi Legislature's official bill-status system confirms no enacted amendment to § 37-41-31 or § 37-41-29 in the 2024, 2025, or 2026 Regular Sessions; SB 2290 of the 2026 Regular Session (which would have amended only §§ 37-41-3, 37-41-13, 37-41-27, and 37-41-47) Died In Committee on 02/03/2026 per the official bill history XML.

districtVariance.value: Unknown. Section 37-41-31 establishes a uniform statewide rule on its face (four-year contract term with a discretionary up-to-four-year extension by board action, plus a competitive sealed-bid rule subject to the exceptional-children/extraordinary-circumstances carve-out). The adversarial pass did not locate (a) a Mississippi Attorney General opinion construing § 37-41-31 differently across districts; (b) a Mississippi Office of the State Auditor finding showing inconsistent application of the four-year contract rule (the Auditor's 2018 Purchase Law Update references school bus purchases under § 37-41-101 but not contract-term variance under § 37-41-31); or (c) a published Mississippi School Boards Association interpretive bulletin describing cross-district variance. The MDE 2021 compilation of Title 37 Chapter 41 republishes the chapter text without interpretive overlay. No affirmative evidence of uniform statewide interpretation (e.g., a definitive MDE bulletin saying "every district shall apply § 37-41-31 in this manner") or of credible cross-district interpretive variance was located; the field is therefore recorded as Unknown rather than "No."

specialEducation.value: YES — different procurement rule (not a different term limit). Mississippi Code § 37-41-31(2) (2024) provides verbatim (confirmed against the MDE compilation): "Private contracts for the transportation of exceptional children, as defined in Section 37-23-3, may be negotiated by the local school board without the necessity of the advertising for or taking of bids. The same may apply under extraordinary circumstances where regular transportation is considered to be impractical. The local school board may negotiate and contract for the transportation described in this paragraph so long as the local school board complies with the school transportation regulations promulgated by the State Board of Education." Section 37-41-29(4) (2024) further provides: "Local school boards are not required to file a copy of any private contract for the transportation of exceptional children or the transportation of children under extraordinary circumstances with the State Board of Education." Effect: for special-education / exceptional-children transportation contracts, (a) competitive sealed bidding under § 37-41-31(1) does NOT apply (the board may negotiate directly), and (b) the contract need not be filed with the State Board of Education. The statute does NOT specify a different maximum term for exceptional-children contracts; the four-year/four-year-extension cap in § 37-41-31(3) appears in a separate paragraph that is not expressly limited to bid-let contracts, but neither does it explicitly extend to negotiated exceptional-children contracts — the question is not resolved on the face of the statute and no AG opinion or MDE bulletin clarifying it was located. IDEA and federal special-education law are not contract/procurement rules; they govern the duty to transport students with IEPs, not the term or procurement of the underlying vendor contract, so federal law does not change the state contracting rules. No separate statutory term limit, rebid rule, or renewal mechanic was located for McKinney-Vento, foster-care, or alternative-program transportation; those would fall under either the regular § 37-41-31(1) bid rule or, where they qualify, the § 37-41-31(2) extraordinary-circumstances exception.
specialEducation.citation: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31(2) (2024); Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-29(4) (2024); definition of exceptional children at Miss. Code Ann. § 37-23-3

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term: 4 years initial + up to 4-year discretionary extension under § 37-41-31(3).
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Some other Mississippi statute (general procurement in Title 31 Chapter 7, the multi-year contract authority in § 37-9-25, or an administrative regulation in Title 7 Part 110) authorizes a longer initial term, more than one extension, or evergreen renewal for pupil-transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Re-opened MDE compilation PDF (https://www.mdek12.org/sites/default/files/Offices/MDE/OA/OSOS/Pupil%20Transportation/pupil_transportation_laws_20211027.pdf) and confirmed verbatim text of § 37-41-31(3). (2) WebSearch for 'Mississippi 37-9-25 school board contracts scholastic year multi-year transportation' — § 37-9-25 governs only superintendents/principals/licensed employees, NOT transportation. (3) Searched § 31-7-13 (Title 31 Public Purchases) — cross-referenced only for advertising procedure; does not displace the school-specific term/extension rule. (4) Downloaded SOS Pupil Transportation Handbook (Title 7 Part 110) via the MASS Superintendents library mirror (https://www.superintendents.ms/virtuallibrary/5nmspupiltransportationhandbook8231320170607160206250858.pdf) — handbook is operational/safety; no contract-term provision overriding statute. (5) Searched for AG opinion construing § 37-41-31 to allow longer term — none located.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. No competing authority found; § 37-41-31(3) is the sole rule on term length, and the second 4-year extension is one-time and discretionary.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End: Conditional — initial sealed-bid required under § 37-41-31(1); discretionary extension up to 4 years without rebid under § 37-41-31(3); negotiated procurement for exceptional children / extraordinary circumstances under § 37-41-31(2).
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either (a) extensions are categorically prohibited and rebidding is always required at term-end, or (b) additional unbidded extensions, sole-source/emergency procurement, cooperative purchasing, or piggyback authority exists that lets the board skip the initial sealed-bid step.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Verbatim re-read of § 37-41-31(1)-(3) in the MDE PDF confirms (a) sealed-bid required for initial award; (b) one extension of up to 4 years permitted at expiration without rebid; (c) negotiated procurement only for exceptional children / extraordinary circumstances. (2) Searched § 31-7-13 sole-source/emergency carve-outs — those apply to general public purchases but the school-specific § 37-41-31 controls for transportation contracts. (3) Searched for cooperative/piggyback authority in MS purchasing law affecting school bus contracts — none found that overrides § 37-41-31. (4) State Auditor 2018 Purchase Law Update (https://www.osa.ms.gov/sites/default/files/Resources/Purchasing/Purchase_Law_Update.pdf) addresses school bus purchases under § 37-41-101 (vehicle purchasing), not § 37-41-31 (transportation contracts).

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics: Single statutory discretionary extension up to 4 years; no automatic/evergreen renewal, no CPI/fuel escalators, no state-agency approval required.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Statute or administrative rule provides for CPI/fuel adjustments, automatic rollover, multiple successive extensions, or State Board of Education approval of renewals.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Verbatim text of § 37-41-31(3) reviewed — only ONE extension of "not more than four (4) years" is authorized; the section is silent on CPI/fuel adjustments and on state-agency approval. (2) § 37-41-29 reviewed — sets contract-content requirements and per-pupil funding cap, but contains no escalator/automatic-renewal provision. (3) Pupil Transportation Handbook (Title 7 Part 110) reviewed — no escalator or renewal-approval rule. (4) Searched for AG opinion on second-or-successive extensions of § 37-41-31 contracts — none located.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. No escalator or successive-extension authority found.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education Differentiation: § 37-41-31(2) authorizes negotiated procurement (no bid) for exceptional-children transportation; § 37-41-29(4) exempts such contracts from State Board filing; no separate term limit specified.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: (a) A separate Mississippi statute or SBE regulation imposes a different term limit on exceptional-children transportation; or (b) § 37-41-31(3)'s four-year/four-year-extension cap is unambiguously inapplicable (or unambiguously applies) to negotiated exceptional-children contracts; or (c) McKinney-Vento/foster-care/alternative-program transportation has its own separate Mississippi procurement rule.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Verbatim re-read of § 37-41-31(2) and § 37-41-29(4) — exemption is from bidding and from State Board filing only; statute does not address term limit for negotiated exceptional-children contracts. (2) Searched Title 37 Chapter 23 (Exceptional Children) and the SBE Pupil Transportation Handbook for a separate term rule — none located. (3) Searched for McKinney-Vento / homeless / foster-care Mississippi-specific contracting rule — none found at the state level (federal McKinney-Vento and Fostering Connections impose duty to transport but not state contracting term rules).
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed (procurement rule differs; term-limit applicability to negotiated exceptional-children contracts is unresolved on the face of the statute — Stage 1 already flagged this uncertainty, which is preserved).
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Maintains High confidence on the procurement difference; flagged uncertainty on whether the 4+4 cap applies to negotiated exceptional-children contracts is preserved verbatim.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: District-Level Variance: Unknown (no affirmative evidence of uniform interpretation or cross-district variance).
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Either (a) credible cross-district variance exists (different districts apply the 4+4 rule differently, or treat the extension as automatic vs. discretionary), or (b) authoritative statewide guidance exists demonstrating uniform interpretation, such that the field should be 'Yes' or 'No' rather than 'Unknown.'
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Searched Mississippi AG opinions for any 37-41-31 interpretation — none found. (2) Searched MDE bulletins and the MS State Auditor purchasing law update — neither addresses district-level variance in § 37-41-31. (3) Searched MS School Boards Association published guidance — none on point. (4) Searched school district board minutes and procurement manuals — none documenting variance or uniform interpretation.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed as Unknown. No credible authoritative evidence either way was located.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Maintains High confidence in the Unknown designation.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness: § 37-41-31 and § 37-41-29 not amended since Laws 2014, ch. 486, HB 72, § 3 (eff. 7/1/2014); SB 2290 (2026) does not amend either section.
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2015-2026 Mississippi session law (other than SB 2290 of 2026) amended § 37-41-31 or § 37-41-29 and the prior research missed it.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: (1) Directly fetched the official MS Legislature bill history XML for SB 2290 of 2026 (https://billstatus.ls.state.ms.us/2026/pdf/history/SB/SB2290.xml) — confirmed: Dead, Died in Committee 02/03/26; affects only §§ 37-41-3, -13, -27, -47 (NOT -29 or -31). (2) Searched the 2024, 2025, and 2026 'allmsrs.xml' all-measures indexes (https://billstatus.ls.state.ms.us/2024-2026/pdf/all_measures/allmsrs.xml) for the code section codes '037-0041-0031' and '037-0041-0029' — ZERO hits in all three sessions, confirming no bill in 2024, 2025, or 2026 sessions affected either section.
challengeMatrix.result: Confirmed. § 37-41-31 and § 37-41-29 are current as of 2026-06-24 and unchanged since 2014.

sourcesReVerified: MDE compilation PDF of Title 37, Chapter 41 (re-fetched via MS Superintendents Association mirror; verbatim text of § 37-41-31(1), (2), (3) and § 37-41-29(1)-(4) confirmed character-for-character against Stage 1 quotes): https://www.mdek12.org/sites/default/files/Offices/MDE/OA/OSOS/Pupil%20Transportation/pupil_transportation_laws_20211027.pdf

MS Legislature official bill history XML for SB 2290 (2026 Regular Session): https://billstatus.ls.state.ms.us/2026/pdf/history/SB/SB2290.xml — confirms 'Dead — Died In Committee 02/03/26'; affects §§ 37-41-3, 37-41-13, 37-41-27, 37-41-47 only; does NOT affect § 37-41-31 or § 37-41-29.

MS Legislature 2024, 2025, 2026 all-measures XML indexes (https://billstatus.ls.state.ms.us/{year}/pdf/all_measures/allmsrs.xml) — zero references to '037-0041-0031' or '037-0041-0029' in any of those sessions.

Mississippi Pupil Transportation Handbook (Title 7 Part 110) (MDE-republished PDF via MS Superintendents Association library: https://www.superintendents.ms/virtuallibrary/5nmspupiltransportationhandbook8231320170607160206250858.pdf) — confirmed as operational/safety handbook; contains no contract-term provision overriding § 37-41-31.

Mississippi Office of the State Auditor, Purchase Law Update (https://www.osa.ms.gov/sites/default/files/Resources/Purchasing/Purchase_Law_Update.pdf) — references school bus purchases under § 37-41-101 but not contract-term variance under § 37-41-31.
sourcesUnreachable: https://advance.lexis.com/container?config=00JAAzNzhjOTYxNC0wZjRkLTQzNzAtYjJlYS1jNjExZWYxZGFhMGYKAFBvZENhdGFsb2cMlW40w5iIH7toHnTBIEP0 — Mississippi Code Public Access via LexisNexis is the OFFICIAL publisher portal under contract with the MS Joint Legislative Committee on Compilation, Revision and Publication; the portal does not provide free section-level deep links and the landing page does not render verbatim section text to non-interactive fetches. Citation grounded via the MDE official compilation PDF (republished from the same official source) and corroborated against the MS Legislature bill-history XML (which uses the section codes 037-0041-0031 and 037-0041-0029).

https://law.justia.com/codes/mississippi/title-37/chapter-41/in-general/section-37-41-31/ — Justia returns HTTP 403 on WebFetch and a Cloudflare interactive challenge on direct curl; used Stage 1 as a pathfinder only. Verbatim text re-verified against the MDE compilation PDF instead.
https://codes.findlaw.com/ms/title-37-education/ms-code-sect-37-41-31/ and /ms-code-sect-37-41-29/ — FindLaw returns HTTP 403 on WebFetch.

https://www.sos.ms.gov/ACCode/00000560c.pdf — SOS Administrative Code PDF for Title 7 Part 110 Pupil Transportation Handbook returned HTTP 403 on direct curl on 2026-06-24; regulation existence confirmed via SOS regulation index and full text re-verified against the MS Superintendents Association library mirror of the MDE-republished handbook.

Mississippi Department of Education direct hosts (mdek12.org sub-paths) returned HTTP 403 on WebFetch on 2026-06-24 (although direct curl with a browser user-agent succeeded for the pupil_transportation_laws_20211027.pdf path).

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (verbatim text re-verified via Tier 4 MDE official compilation)
sourceLedger.citation: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-31 (2024) — Awarding of private transportation contracts
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2024 Mississippi Code (most recent annual edition published by LexisNexis as official publisher). Section history through Laws 2014, ch. 486, HB 72, § 3, eff. 7/1/2014; verified no enacted amendment in 2024, 2025, or 2026 Regular Sessions via the MS Legislature bill-status all-measures XML.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Statutory Citation; Special Education differentiation (subsection (2))
sourceLedger.relevantText: (1) ...the contract for such transportation shall be let to the lowest responsible bidder who is able to furnish a solvent bond... Such contracts shall be awarded upon receipt of sealed bids or proposals after the time and place of letting such contracts and the manner of bidding have been duly advertised in some newspaper published in the county in accordance with the procedures provided in Section 31-7-13(c)... The awarding of all such contracts shall, however, in all respects be subject to the provisions of Section 37-41-29. (2) Private contracts for the transportation of exceptional children, as defined in Section 37-23-3, may be negotiated by the local school board without the necessity of the advertising for or taking of bids. The same may apply under extraordinary circumstances where regular transportation is considered to be impractical... (3) Contracts shall be made for four (4) years, at the discretion of the local school board and shall include the stipulation for the placement of district-identifying signage on all buses under contract, as required under Section 37-41-43. Any and all bids may be rejected. At the expiration of any transportation contract, if the school board believes a route should remain substantially as established and finds that the carrier thereon has rendered efficient and satisfactory services it may extend the contract for not more than four (4) years, subject, however, to the provisions of Section 37-41-29.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (companion section; verbatim text re-verified via Tier 4 MDE official compilation)
sourceLedger.citation: Miss. Code Ann. § 37-41-29 (2024) — Private transportation of pupils shall be replaced by public transportation; continuation of existing or approval of new private contracts under certain circumstances; contents of contract
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: 2024 Mississippi Code edition; no 2024, 2025, or 2026 enacted amendments per the MS Legislature bill-status all-measures XML.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Renewal Mechanics (incorporates by reference into § 37-41-31 extension); contract content; Special Education exemption from State Board filing requirement (subsection (4))
sourceLedger.relevantText: (2) It shall be the duty of the school boards to approve the continuation of present private contracts providing for transportation of school pupils, or to approve new contracts entered into whenever it is deemed that the needs of the pupils can best be served by such method of transportation. However, in no case shall the amount allotted per pupil from state funds for transportation in facilities provided by private contracts exceed the amount allowed per pupil from state funds for public transportation in the same county and district. (3) Before any money is allocated or disbursed from the state transportation funds to any school board which is transporting children under private contracts, written contracts shall be entered into by and between the respective carriers and school boards. A copy of each contract shall be filed with the superintendent of schools. The aforesaid contract shall contain, at a minimum, the cost of services to be provided to include all necessary bus routes as well as the cost of school-approved activities such as field trips, the length of the contract, the terms of the utilization of existing pupil transportation facilities and existing fleet of buses, if applicable, the necessary types and amounts of limits of insurance that will protect the district, to include Workers' Compensation Insurance, and the stipulation for the placement of district-identifying signage on all buses under contract, as required under Section 37-41-43. Certificates of insurance shall provide that a thirty-day prior notice of cancellation will be given to the district. ... (4) Local school boards are not required to file a copy of any private contract for the transportation of exceptional children or the transportation of children under extraordinary circumstances with the State Board of Education.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 — Official state-education-department compilation citing binding authority (verbatim source for statutory text re-verification)
sourceLedger.citation: Mississippi Department of Education, 'Transportation of Pupils — Mississippi Code of 1972, Title 37 Education, Chapter 41' (MDE compilation, 2021-10-27)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Compilation file dated 2021-10-27; verbatim text of §§ 37-41-1 through 37-41-103 republished by MDE; matches the 2024 statutory text confirmed via Stage 1 cross-check and via the official bill-status amendments check (no enacted amendments since 2014).
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Max Contract Term; Rebid Required; Renewal Mechanics; Special Education exemption
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section 37-41-31(3): 'Contracts shall be made for four (4) years, at the discretion of the local school board and shall include the stipulation for the placement of district-identifying signage on all buses under contract, as required under Section 37-41-43. Any and all bids may be rejected. At the expiration of any transportation contract, if the school board believes a route should remain substantially as established and finds that the carrier thereon has rendered efficient and satisfactory services it may extend the contract for not more than four (4) years, subject, however, to the provisions of Section 37-41-29.' Full verbatim text of §§ 37-41-29(1)-(4) and 37-41-31(1)-(3) confirmed character-for-character on adversarial re-fetch (curl with browser UA on 2026-06-24).

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 1 — Codified state statute (general procurement cross-reference)
sourceLedger.citation: Miss. Code Ann. § 31-7-13 (2024) — Bid requirements and exceptions; public auctions (cross-referenced in § 37-41-31(1) for advertising procedure only)
sourceLedger.relevantText: Section 37-41-31(1) cross-references § 31-7-13(c) for the manner and form of newspaper advertising of sealed bids for private pupil-transportation contracts. The general purchasing thresholds and sole-source/emergency carve-outs in § 31-7-13 do not displace the school-specific bid/extension rule in § 37-41-31.

CODESECTIONS affected: 037-0041-0003 (§ 37-41-3), 037-0041-0013 (§ 37-41-13), 037-0041-0027 (§ 37-41-27), 037-0041-0047 (§ 37-41-47). Does NOT touch § 37-41-31 or § 37-41-29. sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Direct SOS PDF returned HTTP 403 on 2026-06-24 (consistent with Stage 1); regulation existence confirmed via SOS regulation index. Full handbook text re-verified via the MS Superintendents Association library mirror of the MDE-republished handbook (https://www.superintendents.ms/virtuallibrary/5nmspupiltransportationhandbook8231320170607160206250858.pdf, 34 pages, HTTP 200, downloaded 2026-06-24). The handbook is operational/safety in nature; no contract-term, bid, extension, or renewal provision supplements or modifies the statutory rule in § 37-41-31.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Implementation context only — handbook governs operational/safety standards. The four-year contract term and bid rule are set by statute (§ 37-41-31), not by the handbook.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Adversarial re-fetch of the handbook (34 pages) found no provision establishing or modifying contract term, bid procedures, extension/renewal mechanics, or special-education contract procurement rules. The only contract/bid references concern bus drivers and operational standards. The statutory rule controls.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: Tier 4 — Official state auditor purchase law update (negative result on transportation contract term)

sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Downloaded HTTP 200 on 2026-06-24; document references school bus purchasing under § 37-41-101 (vehicles) only — does NOT address contract-term variance under § 37-41-31.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: District-Level Variance (negative result); confirms no Auditor finding of inconsistent application of the four-year contract rule.
sourceLedger.relevantText: Auditor's document treats school bus purchases under § 37-41-101 (e.g., 'School Buses Must Also Comply with §37-41-101, A.G. 4/23/01, Richard L. Thompson'); no discussion of contract-term or extension variance under § 37-41-31.

verificationNotes.codeCurrency: Mississippi Code Annotated 2024 edition (LexisNexis, official publisher under contract with the MS Joint Legislative Committee on Compilation, Revision and Publication of Legislation). The MDE 2021-10-27 compilation matches the 2024 text verbatim for § 37-41-31 and § 37-41-29; no amendments enacted in the 2024, 2025, or 2026 Regular Sessions per the MS Legislature bill-status all-measures XML indexes.
verificationNotes.adminCodeCurrency: Mississippi Administrative Code Title 7 (Education K-12), Part 110 (Mississippi Pupil Transportation Handbook). Direct SOS PDF (https://www.sos.ms.gov/ACCode/00000560c.pdf) returned HTTP 403 on 2026-06-24; full text re-verified via MS Superintendents Association mirror (https://www.superintendents.ms/virtuallibrary/5nmspupiltransportationhandbook8231320170607160206250858.pdf). The handbook contains no provision altering the statutory term, bid, or extension rule.
verificationNotes.sessionLawCheck: Mississippi Legislature Bill Status System checked for 2024, 2025, 2026 Regular Sessions (via official allmsrs.xml indexes). Zero bills in any of those sessions reference code sections 037-0041-0031 or 037-0041-0029. SB 2290 (2026) — the only 2026 bill materially addressing private student transportation — Died In Committee 02/03/26 and would not have amended § 37-41-31 or § 37-41-29 (it affected §§ 37-41-3, -13, -27, -47 only). Conclusion: § 37-41-31 and § 37-41-29 are unchanged since Laws 2014, ch. 486, HB 72, § 3.
negativeSearchPaths: District-Level Variance: Searched MS AG opinions, MS Office of the State Auditor purchase law update, MDE bulletins, and MS School Boards Association published guidance for any cross-district interpretation of § 37-41-31. None located. Search concepts: 'Mississippi attorney general opinion 37-41-31', 'Mississippi state auditor school bus contract four years', 'Mississippi school district policy transportation contract four year extend variance'. Result: Unknown (no affirmative evidence either way).
Justia, FindLaw, and Cornell LII pathfinder pages returned HTTP 403 / Cloudflare challenges on 2026-06-24; verbatim text re-verification was performed via the MDE compilation PDF instead.

Mississippi SOS Administrative Code PDF for Title 7 Part 110 (https://www.sos.ms.gov/ACCode/00000560c.pdf) returned HTTP 403 on direct curl on 2026-06-24; regulation existence confirmed via SOS index and full text re-verified via the MS Superintendents Association mirror.

Mississippi LexisNexis Public Access portal (the OFFICIAL Mississippi Code publisher) does not provide free section-level deep links; the portal landing page is the official citation target for code sections. Verbatim text grounding is via the MDE-published official compilation that republishes the same statutory text.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: No conflict between authoritative sources. (1) Mississippi Code § 37-41-31(1)-(3) and § 37-41-29(1)-(4) as published by LexisNexis (the official publisher) and republished verbatim by the Mississippi Department of Education (2021-10-27 compilation), (2) the section-history note (last amended Laws 2014, ch. 486, HB 72, § 3, eff. 7/1/2014), and (3) the Mississippi Legislature's 2024, 2025, and 2026 bill-status all-measures XML indexes (zero amendments to either section) all agree: (a) four-year initial contract term at the discretion of the local school board; (b) one discretionary up-to-four-year extension at expiration without rebid, if the route should remain substantially as established and the carrier rendered efficient and satisfactory services; (c) competitive sealed-bid award required for initial contracts under § 37-41-31(1), subject to advertising procedure in § 31-7-13(c); (d) negotiated procurement permitted for exceptional-children contracts under § 37-41-31(2) and for extraordinary circumstances; (e) special-education contracts exempt from State Board filing per § 37-41-29(4). No conflicting AG opinion located. No conflicting MDE bulletin or SBE rule located.
